ogTitle: Blueys Beach vs Boambee: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blueys Beach and Boambee.
heading: Blueys Beach vs Boambee — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blueys Beach, New South Wales and Boambee, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blueys Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Boambee (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blueys Beach or Boambee safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blueys Beach has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Boambee scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blueys Beach scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Boambee scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blueys Beach or Boambee?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blueys Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
